questions.q: The single most important prerequisite for a niche directory site is:
choices: The founder's prior knowledge of the niche
An existing email list of 10,000 subscribers
questions.explain: Per Specs/ChannelIdentity.md: 'the founder's prior niche advantage is the real moat.' AI amplifies that moat; it doesn't create it. The cheap stack and Kimi K2.7 help, but they don't substitute for knowing the vertical.

questions.q: Why does the order of monetisation matter — paid listings before AdSense?
questions.explain: AdSense requires organic traffic, which takes 6–12 months to build. Paid listings only need a listing owner who wants visibility in front of your audience — which can happen in weeks 4–8. The first dollar should not wait on Google ranking your pages.

questions.q: Roughly how long does the SEO loop take to start producing meaningful traffic?
questions.explain: Per the channel: the build is fast (48 hours with AI); the SEO loop is slow (6–18 months). Most founders who quit do so in months 2–5, just before the loop would have compounded.

questions.q: Per the 50-listings test, what does it mean if you can list 50 businesses from memory?
choices: You are ready to start building the directory today.
You have niche advantage — you've encountered the niche, not researched it.
You should pick a more obscure niche where fewer competitors exist.
You should immediately hire a copywriter to draft descriptions.
questions.explain: Memory implies encounter — you've talked to people in the niche, watched businesses grow, read about them. That's domain expertise, the actual moat. A founder who has to research the niche is a curator, not a domain expert.

questions.q: What does the channel's documented stack for niche directories look like, and roughly what does it cost to start?
questions.explain: Hostinger Horizons ($3–$10/mo) + Kimi K2.7 ($10–$20/mo) + optional Hermes for automation ($5–$10/mo) + a domain ($10–$15/yr) = ~$20–$40/mo total. Cheap stack first, upgrade only when revenue justifies.

questions.q: List the three failure modes for niche directories, in order of frequency.
questions.explain: (1) No niche advantage — the founder picked a niche they didn't know. (2) Premature monetisation flip — AdSense before traffic, waiting for revenue that won't come. (3) Premature abandonment — quitting in months 2–5, just before SEO would have compounded.
